rationale: Baseline iter 0 NLPD=2.1589 used Normal likelihood and linear mean. Data shows clear outliers (±12-17) and nonlinear sinusoidal-like mean. Student-t handles outliers robustly; cubic polynomial should capture the nonlinear arch shape in x=[0,3] region.

notes: Student-t + sine basis: sin(pi*x/3) + cos(pi*x/3) + linear
rationale: Iter 1 NLPD=1.3181 used cubic polynomial. The data arch peaks at x~1.5 and returns to 0 at x~3, matching sin(pi*x/3) naturally. Adding cos term for phase flexibility. Student-t retained from iter 1 for outlier robustness.
Iter 2 NLPD=1.3060 used 1 Fourier harmonic. Adding 2nd harmonic (sin/cos of 2*pi*x/3) to capture finer structure. Keeping Student-t for outlier robustness.

Iter 3 (2nd harmonic) didn't improve over iter 2 NLPD=1.3060. Dataset explicitly mentions heteroscedastic noise. Adding linear log-sigma model to capture variance that changes across x range (two distinct clusters: [0,3] and [3,5] may have different noise levels).
Iter 4 linear log-sigma gave NLPD=1.2952. Adding quadratic term for more flexible variance structure - two clusters [0,3] and [3,5] may have non-monotone variance profile.
Iter 5 NLPD=1.2854. Adding quadratic correction to sine basis to capture residual nonlinearity in the mean. Keeping quadratic log-sigma.

notes: Student-t + learnable frequency omega + quadratic log-sigma
rationale: Iter 5 NLPD=1.2854 used fixed omega=pi/3. Iter 6 quadratic mean correction didn't help. Now freeing omega as a parameter — if true frequency differs from pi/3, this improves mean fit. Prior centered on pi/3 with sd=0.3.

notes: Gaussian outlier mixture (pi_out~10%) + learnable omega + quadratic log-sigma
rationale: Iter 7 NLPD=1.2844 used Student-t. Data shows ~10-15% extreme outliers (±12-17 vs typical ±1-2). Explicit 2-component mixture (inlier Normal + outlier Normal with sigma_out~10) may model this better than uniform heavy tails from Student-t.

notes: Gaussian mixture with fixed sigma_out=10 + learnable omega + quadratic het.
rationale: Iter 8 NLPD=1.2635 but R-hat=1.73 due to mixture multimodality. Fixing sigma_out=10 (data outliers ±12-17) removes label-switching while keeping mixture structure. Only pi_out is estimated.

notes: Gaussian mixture with estimated sigma_out (lognormal prior ~10) + learnable omega + quadratic het.
rationale: Iter 9 NLPD=1.2325 fixed sigma_out=10. Allowing sigma_out to be estimated with lognormal(log(10), 0.5) prior and lower bound >3 to prevent label-switching. Optimal outlier scale may differ from 10.

notes: Mixture (fixed sigma_out=10) + learnable omega + cubic log-sigma
rationale: Iter 9 NLPD=1.2325 best. Iter 10 estimated sigma_out failed. Adding cubic log-sigma term (s3*x^3) to capture more heteroscedasticity across two x clusters [0,3] and [3,5].

Iter 11 NLPD=1.2256 used cubic polynomial log-sigma. Trying sine-based log-sigma: log(sigma)=s0+s_sin*sin(omega*x)+s_cos*cos(omega*x)+s_lin*x. Variance may follow same sinusoidal pattern as mean (larger in high-response region x~1.5).

notes: Mixture (sigma_out=10) + learnable omega + cubic log-sigma + spatially-varying pi_out
rationale: Iter 11 NLPD=1.2256 (best). Iter 12 sine log-sigma didn't help. Keeping cubic log-sigma from iter 11. Adding logit(pi_out(x))=p0+p1*x — outlier probability may vary systematically across x (two distinct clusters [0,3] and [3,5]).

notes: Student-t inlier + Normal(10) outlier mixture + learnable omega + cubic log-sigma
rationale: Iter 11 NLPD=1.2256 best. Iters 12,13 didn't improve (streak=2). Replacing Normal inlier with Student-t(nu) to handle moderate within-class deviations. Extreme outliers (±12-17) handled by Normal(mu,10). Prior nu~gamma(4,0.3) allows moderate-to-heavy tails.
